content.claim: In Hungary, higher socioeconomic status is associated with access to early-selective academic tracks even after controlling for prior test scores, and value-added analysis shows performance divergence across tracks widens between grades 6–10 independent of baseline ability or family background controls.
content.context: Fresh live research through the Education finance lens
limitations: Value-added analysis relies on measured prior achievement; unobserved ability differences may remain
PISA data reflect snapshot cross-sections; causal attribution to tracking requires quasi-experimental design
Socioeconomic status measured through proxy indicators with measurement error
content.population: Population described in the cited finding
content.time_scope: As reported by the cited source
